title: Suspended tile system and absorber tile element
abstract: The present invention relates to an absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) comprising compressed mineral fiber material and having a first major surface (107a-b, 107e-f, 207a), an opposing second major surface (108a-b, 108e-f, 308a) and at least one side edge surface (109a-f, 209a, 309a), and an anchor element (106a-f) associated with one of the first and second major surface or one of the at least one side edge surfaces. The anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) comprises a head portion (110a-b, 110e-f) and a threaded portion (112a-b, 112e-f), the head portion (110a-b, 110e-f) defining an open cavity (114a-b, 114e-f) facing a top surface (116a-b, 116e-f), wherein an engagement member (118a-b, 118e-f) is arranged in the cavity (114a-b, 114e-f), and configured for engagement with a hook element (124a-b, 124e- f). The threaded portion (112a-b, 112e-f) is threaded into the absorber tile element (102a-f) such that the head portion (110a-b, 110e-f) becomes substantially recessed in the absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a). A corresponding tile system is also provided.
claims:
Claims 1 . An absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) comprising compressed mineral fiber material and having a first major surface (107a-b, 107e-f, 207a), an opposing second major surface (108a-b, 108e-f, 308a), and at least one side edge surface (109a-f, 209a, 309a) extending between the first major surface (107a-b, 107e-f, 207a) and the second major surface (108a-b, 108e-f, 308a), an anchor element (106a-f) associated with one of the first major surface (107a-b, 107e-f, 207a) and the second major surface (108a-b, 108e-f, 308a) or one of the at least one side edge surfaces (109a-f, 209a, 309a) of the absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a), wherein the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) comprises a head portion (1 10a-b, 1 10e-f) and a threaded portion (1 12a-b, 1 12e-f), the head portion (1 10a-b, 1 10e-f) defining an open cavity (1 14a-b, 1 14e-f) facing a top surface (1 16a-b, 1 16e-f) of the head portion (1 10a-b, 1 10e-f), wherein an engagement member (1 18a-b, 1 18e-f) is arranged in the cavity (1 14a-b, 1 14e-f), and configured for engagement with a hook element (124a-b, 124e- f). the threaded portion (1 12a-b, 1 12e-f) being threaded into the absorber tile element (102a-f) from the surface (107a-b, 107e-f; 108a-b, 108e-f; 209a; 309a) associated with the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) such that the head portion (1 10a- b, 1 10e-f) becomes substantially recessed in the absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a). 2. The absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) according to claim 1 , wherein the engagement member (1 18a, 1 18e-f) comprises an elongated bar structure traversing the cavity (1 14a, 1 14e-f) of the head portion (1 10a, 1 1 Oe- f)Â· 3. The absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) according to claim 1 , wherein the engagement member (118b) comprises a loop structure arranged in the cavity (114b) of the head portion (110b). 4. The absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) according to claim 1 , wherein the engagement member (118a-b, 118e-f) is laterally displaced with respect to a central axis (CA) of the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a). 5. The absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) according to claim 1 , wherein the anchor element (106a, 106c-f, 206a, 306a) is provided with a through hole (122a, 122e-f) extending along a central axis (CA) of the anchor element (106a, 106c-f, 206a, 306a). 6. The absorber tile element (100, 200, 300) according to claim 1 , wherein the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) has an extension in the interval of 10-150 mm, preferably 15-100 mm, along a central axis (CA) thereof. 7. The absorber tile element (100, 200, 300) according to claim 1 , wherein the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) has an extension in the interval of 10-100 mm, preferably 15-60 mm, in a direction transverse to a central axis (CA) thereof. 8. The absorber tile element according to claim 1 , wherein the threaded portion (112a-b, 112e-f) of the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) comprises threads (120a-b, 120e-f) having a pitch in the interval of 4-10 mm, preferably 5-7 mm. 9. A tile system (100, 200, 300) comprising: an absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a) comprising an anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) according to any one of claims 1 -8, and 

 a suspension element (104a-104f, 104x, 204a, 304a) releasably coupled to the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a) for suspension of the absorber tile element (102a-f, 202a, 302a), wherein the suspension element (104a-104f, 104x, 204a, 304a) comprises a hook element (124a-b, 124e-f) hooking the engagement member (118a-b, 118e-f) of the anchor element (106a-f, 206a, 306a). 10. The tile system (100, 200, 300) according to claim 9, wherein the engagement member (118a-b, 118e-f) and/or the hook element (124a-b, 124e-f) is/are configured to align a body of the suspension element (104a- 104f, 104x, 204a) with a central axis (CA) of the anchor element (106a-f).
